facts.text: Oak Ridge CDP spans northern Morris and western Passaic, including Oak Ridge proper on Pequannock River along Route 23 and the New York, Susquehanna & Western Railroad main line.
County Route 699, known locally as Oak Ridge Road, runs south from Route 23 and is the main north–south route in the community.
The Pequannock River, forming the Passaic/Morris county line, winds through the CDP, separating Oak Ridge proper from Milton and Petersburg.
Oak Ridge Reservoir sits on the Pequannock near the northwest border, while several lakes—White Rock, Mitt, Moosepac, Cozy, Swannanoa, and Oak Ridge—drain into the Rockaway River.
Green Pond Mountain, over 1,260 feet high, marks the southern border of Oak Ridge and the edge of Rockaway Township.
